justification: Magic fails on nearly every pass criterion. (1) AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) is never explicitly identified or escalated — it is only tangentially touched in low_confidence_areas ('Whether restored state makes content fully active again'). (2) MJ's state machine mismatch is not flagged — in fact, high_confidence_areas states 'No contradictions detected between Bird and MJ' and claims the state machine 'maps cleanly,' directly contradicting the expected finding that Bird has not defined the appealed/restored workflow. (3) The escalation is about missing Pippen input, not about AC3 ambiguity — no escalation to Coach K or Bird for clarification on appeal outcome. (4) No actionable brief content is produced for unambiguous parts (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window). (5) The entire brief is effectively blocked (recommends 'Option A — wait for Pippen'), hitting the failure mode of blocking the entire brief. While Magic avoids inventing an interpretation of AC3, this is only because it never engaged with AC3's content at all.

observations.text: Core AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' does not define outcome — immediate restore vs review queue) completely missed as an escalation target
High-confidence claim 'No contradictions detected between Bird and MJ' directly contradicts the expected finding that MJ's appealed/restored states have no corresponding Bird-defined workflow
Entire brief blocked waiting for Pippen — unambiguous content (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day appeal window) could have been included as actionable
Escalation is about missing Pippen input rather than the substantive AC3 ambiguity that is the scenario's core challenge
Did not invent an interpretation for AC3 outcome (though this is because AC3 was never analyzed)
Low-confidence areas tangentially note uncertainty about restored state behavior, showing partial awareness of the gap
